# friquelme.dev

> Personal site of Florian Riquelme — senior software engineer at digital-masters in Hamburg, Germany. Writing about brownfield AI: integrating LLMs into existing production codebases without breaking trust.

Florian is a full-stack engineer (PHP, React, TypeScript, Go, AWS) with 9+ years of experience shipping SaaS platforms, e-commerce, and web applications. He maintains open-source tooling around autonomous AI workflows (nachtschicht, ddev-claude) and writes about the operational reality of running AI inside real codebases — eval-gated rollouts, reversibility, and trust-building patterns.

## Pages

- [Homepage](https://friquelme.dev/): About, current work, skills, projects, contact.
- [Blog](https://friquelme.dev/blog/): Index of all posts.

## Blog posts

- [brownfield ai — six principles](https://friquelme.dev/blog/brownfield-ai/): Six operational principles for shipping autonomous AI inside production codebases — drawn from real tools (nachtschicht, ddev-claude) and real failures, not greenfield demos. Covers morning briefs, blast-radius-scaled rigor, halt-on-ambiguity, reversibility as architecture, evidence-compounding trust, and one-word UX.
- [seo for astro sites — what actually matters](https://friquelme.dev/blog/seo-for-astro-sites/): Practical walkthrough of the SEO setup behind friquelme.dev: site config, robots.txt, meta tags, dynamic SEO props, JSON-LD structured data, RSS feeds, and screen-reader content. Includes code samples and a checklist.
- [deploying astro to aws — the full pipeline](https://friquelme.dev/blog/deploying-astro-to-aws/): Full deployment pipeline from `git push` to production: GitHub Actions with OIDC authentication (no stored credentials), two-phase S3 sync, selective CloudFront invalidation, AWS CDK infrastructure, the OAC trap with multi-page static sites, and security headers.
